nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO & PADDY JAZZ
nts1.d: First thing on Fridays - Jack Rollo's here to take care of things.

nts1.n: The NTS Breakfast Show w/ TASH LC
SOUP TO NUTS W/ SCARY THINGS
nts1.d: Friday mornings get scary - Bempah & JK elevating your vibe right in time for the weekend.
Special guest shows from around the world.
Cousins crew Basmati, Zaleel, A.G and Manara tear up the studio every other month.
Regular techno, electro, and house missives from the Against Interpretation labelhead and powerhouse-club selector Courtesy…
Skee's insight through his daily listening habits, from guilty pleasures to casual recommendations and in between…

nts1.n: UP YA ARCHIVES w/ Chinese Daughter
nts1.d: Star UK producer Nia Archives takes over the NTS airwaves in a weekly residency, celebrating the launch of Up Ya Archives, the new record label playing host to the best of new gen junglism.

Martha is a DJ and broadcaster from South London with roots in community radio and documentary making. Each of Martha’s thoughtfully curated radio shows navigate the fringes of electronic music, with a clear narrative that is grounded in community and storytelling.
Serving up monthly discoveries in clubland and beyond.

nts1.n: EASTERN MARGINS - Dirty K ☆ 台客無間斷 RAVE MIX
nts1.d: Regular transmissions from the London crew showcasing music from the margins of East & South-East Asia and its diaspora.
The enigmatic polymath, producer and MC brbko takes over the NTS airwaves each month. Brak don't crack.

nts1.n: CDMX TAPES W/ LUCA DURAN & OCTOPTIC (R)
Nāda Sonics w/ Sway of the Verses, Prattyush Banerjee & Giuliano Modarelli
THE ONE GLOVE BREAKFAST SHOW W/ MACCA
Highlife & Sunshine Show w/ Tryb & ZENA

nts2.d: Shanghai, China collective 626 Company, headed up by Jackzebra deliver monthly broadcasts from the collective and their friends.

pop.soil is Lyra Pramuk’s bimonthly series exploring catharsis, transformation, and healing through sonic storytelling.
From the host: "Starting in 1968, Opałka began to supplement the project with recordings of his voice uttering the numbers he was painting at that moment. (…) Opałka planned that at the end of his life, he would reach the number 7777777. He died in 2011, having reached around 5,604,000."
Los Angeles producer & sound artist claire rousay journeys through healing plaintive electronics, quotidian field recordings & minimalist compositions.

Juana Molina is one of South America's most remarkable and exploratory popular musicians. For over three decades, the Argentine-born artist has approached songwriting and music production with a childlike curiosity and sage open-mindednes, garnering a beloved following in Argentina and beyond. 
Her music, which often compared to Björk, is a patchwork of folk, ambient, electronic and indie rock, and avant-garde in a way that is truly her own. Listening to Molina's music, which is often suffused with humour and oddity, it perhaps comes as no surprise that she first rose to prominence as a comedian, featuring on Artgentinian sketch comedy shows in the early 1990s.

DJ: “… I don’t know, I didn’t… I didn’t think I’d be the one holding the air, I never spoke live before the event consumed the many. I’ve no news for the desperate survivors and… and I can’t… well, I can hope that this next song might help us survive a little longer…"
From the host: "DJ: “On the theme of love, caller on line 8. Tell us a small joy.”
Line 8: “Sealing a kiss.”
DJ: “Stealing?”
Line 8: “Seal; to close so tightly that no utterances can escape.”
DJ: “To create a void?”
Line 8: “To master a cavern of sound, without the need for words.”"
Out-there sounds from rusia-IDK, the Madrid-based crew, hosting some of the most exciting producers and artists coming out of Spain and Europe.
PAM & Satoshi from the blog Okonkole Y Trompa select 2 hours of records from their collection for NTS.

Andy Butler from Hercules & Love Affair joins us once a month to showcase his diverse record collection.
From the host: "My first chance to play one of the three remixes Larry Heard did for a forthcoming remix EP- It's got some classic acid, some current faves, some new artists…"

Antropoceno explores the main trends of Brazilian experimental music - each episode will dive into a different subgenre. This is an effort to catalogue Brazil\s contemporary scene and contextualize it in the history of Brazilian avant-garde, paying homage to the records that paved the way to influence and build the sounds we see today.
From the host: "In today's episode, we'll dive into the scene of Brazilian Avant-Garde Rock and Metal - starting from the early experimentations from "Os Mutantes" up until the contemporary artists who are still challenging conceptions and expanding the boundaries of the genre."

nts2.n: CONFUSING MIX W/ JOSH DA COSTA
nts2.d:
Lock in for a monthly transmission of leftfield sonics from Josh Da Costa live from LA.
From the host: "Mostly a post-tour recap of a recent trip through UK & Europe ! A few of my recent weird finds."

Tana Yonas collects songs like souvenirs, each recalling precious moments found leafing through dusty foreign record shops or catching sonic fragments weathered by time from the radio of bumpy taxi rides. Listen as she delicately guilds together these memories and shares music from lesser known and under represented musical traditions from across the globe and decades.

nts2.n: Improvisational Aesthetics w/ LAGOS (Leo123 x RADIUS)
nts2.d: Chicago DJ, producer and performers RADIUS and Leo 123 present their take on jazz, taking in the breadth of the genre, from free jazz to modal to Latin styles and more.

nts2.n: Sacred Space: Spring Special w/ Radha
ROOMIES W/ MOBILEGIRL - MUSIC FOR THE STUDIO (R)
EDGE OF THE FOREST: Fish String Theory
LIQUID MIRROR W/ OLIVE KIMOTO - RYUICHI SAKAMOTO TRIBUTE (R)
Sounds on Screen: Paul Thomas Anderson

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y
Mid Morning Matters w/ Alfie Danger
Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

dublab.n: Daddy Differently and Dirty Dave - Things of Life
dublab.d: It’s the simple things isn’t it? The little things of life. Good food, sunshine, no traffic, dark coffee, 72˚ breeze, telephone off the hook and music every Friday morning a la Daddy Differently & Dirty Dave.
Peak Oil records presents ‘tipping point’; a monthly journey through the record collections of brian and brion. Leaving no good genre unturned, enjoy two hours of esoterica, conversation and guests.

dublab.n: Ill Camille x Jesse Fairfax - The Sweeter the Juice
Greg Belson - Divine Chord Gospel Show
If You Know You Know – guest session
rerun: Peak Oil w/ ML Buch — Tipping Point
In Sheep's Clothing – World of Sound
Dustin Wong – Why Did Why Did Why Did

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy
